016package org.consensusj.bitcoinj.signing;
017
018import org.bitcoinj.base.Address;
019import org.bitcoinj.core.TransactionInput;
020import org.bitcoinj.crypto.ECKey;
021import org.bitcoinj.core.Transaction;
022import org.bitcoinj.core.TransactionOutPoint;
023import org.bitcoinj.core.TransactionOutput;
024import org.bitcoinj.crypto.DeterministicKey;
025import org.bitcoinj.script.ScriptBuilder;
026import org.bitcoinj.wallet.DeterministicKeyChain;
027
028import java.util.List;
029import java.util.Optional;
030import java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture;
031
032/**
033 * A "signing wallet"  that uses a {@link DeterministicKeyChain} to
034 * sign {@link SigningRequest}s.
035 */
036public class HDKeychainSigner implements BaseTransactionSigner {
037    private final DeterministicKeyChain keyChain;
038
039    public HDKeychainSigner(DeterministicKeyChain keyChain) {
040        this.keyChain = keyChain;
041    }
042
043    /**
044     * Create a signed bitcoinj transaction from the signing request
045     * <p>
046     * NOTE: This was an attempt to use an unsigned transaction and a list of address.
047     * It is private and should not be used. I'm leaving it here because it demonstrates
048     * the use of `duplicateDetached()` and we may want to use this elsewhere to sign
049     * incomplete bitcoinj transactions.
050     * <p>
051     * Assumes all inputs are P2PKH (for now) and addresses must be in
052     * the same order as the unsigned inputs in the unsigned transaction.
053     * 
054     * @param addresses An ordered list of funding addresses (keys must be in HD keychain)
055     * @param unsignedTx An unsigned bitcoinj transaction
056     * @return a newly created, signed bitcoinj transaction
057     */
058    private CompletableFuture<Transaction> signTransaction(List<Address> addresses, Transaction unsignedTx) {
059        if (addresses.size() != unsignedTx.getInputs().size()) {
060            throw new IllegalArgumentException("addresses and inputs must be 1:1 mapped");
061        }
062
063        // Create a new, empty bitcoinj transaction
064        Transaction tx = new Transaction();
065
066        // For each output in the signing request, add an output to the bitcoinj transaction
067        // TODO: Transaction validation
068        for (TransactionOutput output : unsignedTx.getOutputs()) {
069            tx.addOutput(output.duplicateDetached());
070        }
071
072        // For each address in the input list, add a signed input to the bitcoinj transaction
073        for (int index = 0; index < unsignedTx.getInputs().size(); index++) {
074            Address address = addresses.get(index);
075            TransactionInput input = unsignedTx.getInputs().get(index);
076            TransactionOutPoint outPoint = input.getOutpoint().disconnectOutput();
077            DeterministicKey fromKey = keyChain.findKeyFromPubHash(address.getHash());
078            tx.addSignedInput(outPoint, ScriptBuilder.createOutputScript(address), input.getValue(), fromKey);
079        }
080        return CompletableFuture.completedFuture(tx);
081    }
082
083    /**
084     * Return the signing key for an input, if available
085     * @param input Transaction input data
086     * @return Signing key, if available, {@link Optional#empty()} otherwise.
087     */
088    public Optional<ECKey> keyForInput(TransactionInputData input) {
089
090        return Optional.ofNullable(
091                keyChain.findKeyFromPubHash(input.script().getPubKeyHash())
092        );
093    }
094
095    /**
096     * Return the signing key for an input, if available
097     * @param pubKeyHash pubKeyHash
098     * @return Signing key, if available, {@link Optional#empty()} otherwise.
099     */
100    public Optional<ECKey> keyForHash(byte[] pubKeyHash) {
101
102        return Optional.ofNullable(
103                keyChain.findKeyFromPubHash(pubKeyHash)
104        );
105    }
106
107    public Optional<ECKey> pubKeyFromPubKeyHash(byte[] pubKeyHash) {
108        return keyForHash(pubKeyHash).map(ECKey::fromPublicOnly);
109    }
110}
